Use environment variables prefixed with NEXT_PUBLIC_ only for values that must be exposed to the browser
Store sensitive keys and secrets in server-only environment variables (no NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix)
Define environment variable schemas and validate them at build time using libraries like @t3-oss/env-nextjs or zod
Use TypeScript const assertions or enums for route path constants to avoid magic strings
Co-locate component-specific constants within the component file or a nearby constants file
Use camelCase for local variables and UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for module-level constants
Leverage Next.js built-in environment variable loading from .env, .env.local, .env.production, and .env.development files
Type all props and state variables with explicit TypeScript interfaces or types
Use React.useState with explicit generic types when the initial value doesn't convey the full type
Prefer 'as const' for configuration objects that should be deeply immutable
